title: Ysanne Isard (clone)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Ysanne Isard (clone) |homeworld=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' |birth=Around [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', below Coruscant |death=[[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], [[Ciutric IV]]''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |donor=[[Ysanne Isard]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' |height=1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]Based on [[Ysanne Isard]]'s height and skin [[Color/Legends|color]], provided in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''. |mass= |hair=[[Black]] and [[white]] |feathers= |eyes=Left: [[red]], right: [[blue]] |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Ciutric Hegemony]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I appreciate my foes and their abilities. Then again, I would hope a clone would be an improvement over the original.''"
"''What?''"
"''You didn't know you were a clone? No, of course not. Isard wouldn't trust the dispersal of her prized captives to just anyone: She gave the job to herself. With you she could actually be in two places at one time.''"
"''That's insane.''"
"''So was she.|Wedge Antilles and the clone of Ysanne Isard|}} Around [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]], using a [[Spaarti cloning cylinder]] from the [[Death/Legends|late]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Palpatine's Spaarti cloning facility|storage]], [[Cloning/Legends|grew]] a '''clone''' of [[Gender/Legends|herself]] aboard her [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', which was hidden beneath the surface of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Isard constantly updated the memories of the clone, who was kept in a state of [[Suspended animation/Legends|suspended animation]], to ensure she would believe herself to be the real Isard upon awakening. After the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|conquered]] Coruscant with the help of the elite [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] unit [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], Isard [[Mission above Imperial City|retreated]] to the planet [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], establishing a regime there. During the [[Bacta War]] against Rogue [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]] in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Isard activated her clone and sent her out on a mission to scatter the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] of the ''Lusankya'', whom Rogue Squadron sought to rescue. Isard planned to kill the clone when she returned; however, before she was able do so, Isard was [[Battle of Thyferra|overthrown]] and went into hiding, and the clone was left alone in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. [[9 ABY/Legends|Two years]] later, the clone joined with [[Prince-Admiral/Legends|Prince-Admiral]] [[Delak Krennel]] in the [[Ciutric Hegemony]], giving him [[Politics/Legends|political]] advice during his [[Hegemony theater|fight]] against the Republic and devising a complicated plan to destroy Rogue Squadron. By planting a series of clues to the supposed location of the [[Prison/Legends|prisoners]], she lured the squadron to [[Distna]], a hollow [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in the [[Corvis Minor system/Legends|Corvis Minor system]], where a large force of starfighters provided by Krennel ambushed them. Unknown to the clone, the real Isard had been helping her to lure Rogue Squadron into the trap all the way, only to rescue them in the end. By doing so, she had earned their trust and employed her former enemies against the clone herself. Posing as the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]]–comprised [[Requiem Squadron]], the Rogues infiltrated [[Ciutric IV]], the capital planet of the Hegemony and launched an [[Battle of Ciutric|attack]] to rescue the prisoners, while the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic fleet]] battled Krennel's forces above the planet. The clone forced [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Lorrir/Legends|Lorrir]] to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] a [[Imperial Assault Shuttle|shuttle]] and attempt to destroy the [[Ciutric IV prison|prison facility]] where the prisoners were being held, while she hid inside a [[Daplona base training center|training center]] and rerouted her [[Comm unit/Legends|comm system]] to Lorrir's [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] in order to appear as though she was aboard the shuttle herself. The attack failed, and [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] saw through her deception when he recognized Lorrir's piloting style. Antilles's men shot down Lorrir, and Antilles himself fired a pair of [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] at the training center, killing the clone. ==Biography== ===Creation=== {{Quote|After I had taken control of Thyferra and you began your campaign to oust me, I realized that if you succeeded, I wished to rob you of the goal you truly sought: the prisoners from my Lusankya. I decided to scatter them. This was a mission I felt I could entrust to no one—it was one I wanted to handle myself, but I was needed on Thyferra. What I did was activate a clone of myself, lead her to believe she was me, and charge her with the task of scattering the Lusankya prisoners. When she returned to Thyferra with her task complete, I had her killed—or so I thought.|Ysanne Isard, to Wedge Antilles|}} [[File:Isard.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Ysanne Isard, the creator of the clone]] The [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of the [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] was grown by Isard herself around [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Soon after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], Isard found the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] [[Palpatine's Spaarti cloning facility|personal cloning facility]] inside the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. She destroyed all of the [[Spaarti cloning cylinder]]s except for one, which she transferred to the ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', her personal [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] hidden beneath the [[Manarai Mountains|Manarai Mountain]] district of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. On the ''Lusankya'', Isard secretly bred a single clone of herself with an intention to employ her when matters needed Isard's personal supervision, but the [[Clone template/Legends|original]] Isard was unavailable to attend to them. To this end, the Director kept her double in a state of [[Suspended animation/Legends|suspended animation]], all the while constantly updating her memories so that upon awakening the clone believed herself to be the real Isard. In late [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—thanks to the efforts of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], its elite [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] unit—[[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|captured Coruscant]].''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' Isard was forced to [[Mission above Imperial City|retreat]] from the planet with the ''Lusankya'' and the still [[Sleep/Legends|sleeping]] clone aboard to the planet [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], where she became the planet's [[Head of State/Legends|ruler]].''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' Just prior to her departure from Coruscant, the Director of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Intelligence]] had her clone's memories updated one last time. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Rogue [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]]—against the will of the New Republic—launched a [[Bacta War|campaign]] against Isard, intending to overthrow her Thyferran regime. The Rogues' other goal was to rescue [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] held aboard the ''Lusankya''; among those [[Prison/Legends|prisoners]] was the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]].''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' Isard wanted to prevent the liberation of the prisoners and decided to scatter them across the galaxy, a task she only entrust to herself. Isard was needed on Thyferra, however, and so she decided to assign the dispersing of the prisoners to her clone; she planned to dispose of the clone afterward by [[Death/Legends|killing]] her, believing that there was a place for only one Ysanne Isard in the galaxy. The clone was finally brought to life and dispatched on her mission. When the clone returned with her task complete, Isard wanted to kill her, but was not able to do so as at that time Rogue Squadron [[Battle of Thyferra|attacked Thyferra]]. Isard was overthrown and presumed to have perished in battle. ===Allying with Krennel=== {{Quote|You do have a purpose here, then?''"
"''The same purpose as always: the preservation of my master's Empire.|Prince-Admiral Delak Krennel and Ysanne Isard's clone, upon their meeting on Ciutric|[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge (abridged audiobook)|''X-Wing: Isard's Revenge'' abridged audiobook]]|audio=Krennel-Isardclone.flac}} [[File:DelakKrennel.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Delak Krennel, an ally of Isard's clone]] Isard had in fact survived, as had her clone, who had no memories of the events on Thyferra as a result of a head trauma she had sustained. Because of the same trauma, the clone was forced to surgically repair her face, and it became asymmetrical and bore a network of raised scars below her right temple. After the loss of Thyferra, both Isards went into hiding, each unaware of the other's existence. While the Republic [[Hunt for Zsinj|was preoccupied]] [[Thrawn campaign|with fighting]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] breakaway [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] like [[Zsinj]] and [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], both Isard and her clone kept a low profile and formulated their own plans to strike back at the Republic and Rogue Squadron. During the Thrawn campaign, the clone gathered the ''Lusankya'' prisoners back up and had them transferred to a [[Xenovet Commenor facility|facility]] that belonged to the [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineering]] corporation [[Xenovet]] on the planet [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]]. Prior to leaving them on Commenor, however, the clone had been moving the prisoners from one location to another, but had been keeping the interior of their cells, the food, and the prison staff the same, so that the prisoners believed that they had been held at the same place all the time. At some point, the prisoners were stationed on [[Distna]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in the [[Corvis Minor system/Legends|Corvis Minor system]], where the bodies of those of them who had died were buried. While the prisoners were being kept at the Commenor facility, the clone paid for their housing to Xenovet's trustee, the attorney [[Mem Wooter]], from her financial institutions also in the Corvis Minor [[Star system/Legends|system]]. Eventually, Xenovet went bankrupt and the clone took direct control of the facility. After the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|defeat]] of Thrawn in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Last Command]]'', which include the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|defeat]] of [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], to [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. the clone received word from her contacts on Coruscant that the [[New Republic Provisional Council]] had decided to strike at one of the Imperial warlords, thus convincing the others that the Republic had not been weakened by Thrawn. The Council chose [[Prince-Admiral/Legends|Prince-Admiral]] [[Delak Krennel]], the ruler of the [[Ciutric Hegemony]], as their target. Krennel had previously allied himself with the real Isard after she promised to make him the new Emperor if he brought her the [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]].[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 33|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 33]] Krennel, however, had murdered Pestage[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] and claimed his holdings, the Ciutric Hegemony, for himself. Possessing the memories of those events and the knowledge of the Republic's plans, the clone Isard made her way to the planet [[Ciutric IV]], the capital of the Hegemony, and offered Krennel an alliance. She provided him with a [[Politics/Legends|political]] program that would crumble the Republic and once again promised to make him the new Emperor, on the condition that he would provide her with resources needed to destroy Rogue Squadron. Believing that the clone was the real Isard, Krennel agreed to the alliance on such terms. ===Schemes and machinations=== {{Quote|This is unacceptable. I will not tolerate the loss of even one world of mine! You have been here for two weeks, have requisitioned a fortune in equipment, have authorized payment to agents all over, and so far have only succeeded in losing personnel and turning prisoners over to the New Republic. This is no way to deal with our enemies. ''[…]''''"
"''I would have thought the lesson Grand Admiral Thrawn learned so recently would not have been lost upon you, Prince-Admiral. ''[…]'' Thrawn died because it was inconceivable to him that anyone could defeat him. While his string of victories made this attitude warranted, this belief also hampered him. ''[…]'' Look at the New Republic. They killed the Emperor. They took Imperial Center. They destroyed Thrawn. Now they believe they are invincible. The fact is, we will defeat them because they have this weakness.|Delak Krennel and the clone|}} The clone's scheme was based on the fact that ever since its creation, the New Republic had been aiming at providing equality between [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and other [[Species/Legends|species]]. This meant, however, that many Humans were losing their wealth and power toward [[Non-huMan|non-Humans]]. Acting on his new ally's advice, Krennel began a pro-Human political program, meant to make the Hegemony appear as a safe-haven for those who did not wish to share their wealth with [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] and felt abused by the Republic. This way, if the Republic were to attack him, it would look as though the aliens had enough influence on the Council to raise arms against someone merely protecting his own species. According to the clone's plan, the program would provoke further fear in Humans and might even force Imperial warlords to unite and become a bigger threat to the Republic's stability. Additionally, Krennel debunked the charges against him via the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]], lying that by killing Pestage he had only done what the New Republic had wanted to do all along. Meanwhile, the clone began to leave a long trail of clues to the supposed location of the prisoners, a trail that eventually led the Rogues to Distna, where a large force provided by Krennel ambushed them. [[File:Commenor-FF88.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Commenor, the site of the Xenovet facility, where the clone of Ysanne Isard sent the prisoners of the ''Lusankya''.]] While creating that trail, Isard's clone tried to leave only the slightest hints for the Rogues in order to appear as though she was in fact trying to prevent them from finding the prisoners. Having struck the deal with Krennel, the clone had most of the prisoners transferred from Commenor to Ciutric, leaving the others to die from starvation. She personally visited the Commenor facility and told the remaining prisoners that they were going to die. The clone also had the dead bodies on Distna dug up and re-buried at a graveyard near the Xenovet facility. She then [[Renewal|erased the memory]] of one of the living prisoners, [[Urlor Sette]], and placed a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] implant containing a deadly [[Poison/Legends|toxin]] developed in the Xenovet facility inside his [[Brain/Legends|brain]]. Sette was acquainted with [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], a Rogue Squadron [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who had managed to escape from the ''Lusankya'' while it had been stationed on Coruscant. The clone programmed the implant to release the toxin inside Sette's brain upon Horn pronouncing Sette's name, and she sent the prisoner with a "message" to Horn at a party thrown in Rogue Squadron's honor on Coruscant. When Sette arrived at the party, and Horn informed the amnesiac man of his name, the toxin was released, killing the prisoner. Sette's appearance hinted at Isard's survival to the Rogues; they soon discovered the cause of the man's death and the origin of the implant, leading them to initiate an [[Mission to Commenor (9 ABY)|attack]] on the Xenovet facility. The clone's plan involved Rogue Squadron finding the prisoners on Commenor dead; she believed that they would also find the graveyard and analyze the state of the bodies buried there. The results of the analysis revealed that the bodies were unable to have decayed so much in Commenor's soil, and the clone believed that the Republic would work up a profile of soil composition needed to accomplish the decay and leave the correct trace elements on the [[Bone/Legends|bones]]—the soil composition of Distna. During the attack, Rogue Squadron liberated some of the prisoners who had managed to survive. The clone had expected the attack to follow later, when all prisoners would have been dead, but even with some of them still alive, she was not concerned, as she believed that they knew nothing beyond what she had wanted them to know, namely, that Isard was alive. The clone believed that since the Republic was now aware of the fact that she was still at large, the Republic's command would theorize that she had allied with Krennel. To this end, she told Krennel to begin negotiations with [[Alderaan Alliance|the refugees]] from the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] planet of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] on the subject of providing them with a world in the Hegemony to settle on, but not to specify which planet just yet. She also instructed him to make a public announcement, apologizing for the Empire's destruction of the planet. When the Republic would attack a world in the Hegemony, Krennel was to state that it was the same planet he had been willing to give to the [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanians]], a statement that would cause even more infighting in the Republic. Although Krennel was initially reluctant to surrender one of his worlds, the clone convinced him that it was necessary to do so to achieve victory in the long term. Soon enough, the Republic [[Hegemony theater|attacked]] the Hegemony, trying to destabilize Krennel's regime. When the Republic had [[Battle of Liinade III|conquered]] the planet [[Liinade III]], Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] stated that a [[Liinade III research station|research station]] run by Krennel had been found there, containing information about a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] similar to the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], the [[Pulsar Station]], that was being built by the Prince-Admiral somewhere in his Hegemony. Neither Isard's clone nor Krennel were aware of the facility's existence. The clone sent her agents to check the reported site of the laboratory, but it had already been removed, and they found only a hole in the ground at the place where it was supposed to be. Since there was no actual evidence to connect the facility to Krennel, the clone dismissed the news as Republic propaganda against him, but did not hesitate to use those news to her advantage, hoping to tie the Pulsar Station into her intrigue as yet another lead to Distna. ===A fateful misconception=== {{Quote|So you are suggesting that no news from the New Republic indicates that your ambush was wholly successful?''"
"''I think that conclusion is warranted.|Krennel and the clone, discussing the ambush|}} The Prince-Admiral, on his ally's instructions, dismissed the charges regarding his involvement in the project in addition to stating that Liinade III had been chosen as the safe-haven for the Alderaanians. In the meantime, the clone released a series of files that supposedly showed that Krennel did not have the resources in the Hegemony to build the Pulsar Station. One of them, however, the Corvis Minor file, had been tampered with by the clone to show the exact opposite. The clone promised Krennel that once Rogue Squadron was destroyed, the Republic would conduct a major strike in the Corvis Minor system, leaving Liinade III open for a counterattack. Such a counterattack would show that Krennel was capable of maintaining the integrity of his state and would increase his popularity. [[File:Rogue_Squadron.svg|thumb|right|250px|The insignia of Rogue Squadron, Isard's sworn enemies]] The clone was unaware that the research facility on Liinade III actually belonged to the real Isard, who had discovered that her double had survived and was plotting her own agenda against the clone. Isard saw through the clone's plans and was planning to rescue Rogue Squadron from the clone's ambush in order to gain their trust to ally with them, and to use the squadron against her clone. Isard, however, believed that the trail the clone had left was too complicated, and she needed to ensure that the Rogues would fall into the trap, so she had faked the files pertaining to the non-existent Pulsar Station in the facility on Liinade III. Since Distna was a hollow moon in the relatively uncharted Corvis Minor system, Rogue Squadron concluded that Krennel was building the station inside the moon. The squadron fell into the trap and embarked on a [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] mission to Distna to check if the superweapon truly existed. There, they were [[Battle of Distna|ambushed]] by six squadrons of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]], and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]]. Unbeknownst to the clone, they were rescued by the real Isard's [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defenders]] under the command of [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Broak Vessery]] and escorted to Isard's secret hideout. The clone's entire campaign of machinations resulted in the destruction of the starfighters provided by Krennel, and the deaths of two [[Noob|rookie]] Rogue Squadron pilots, [[Lyyr Zatoq]] and [[Khe-Jeen Slee]]. Two more Rogues, [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] and [[Asyr Sei'lar]], were initially presumed dead, but were later recovered from the site of the battle with severe injuries that were treated. When Krennel and the clone did not receive word from the ambushing force, the Prince-Admiral instructed the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Aspiration (Victory-class)|''Aspiration'']] stationed in the system to check the site of the battle. The ''Aspiration'' found nothing, although it reported that another [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] had visited the battlefield, but the ''Aspiration''{{'s}} [[Unidentified Aspiration captain|captain]] had not contacted it, believing it to be one of Krennel's [[Starship/Legends|ships]] on a mission connected with the ambush. As the only people who possessed Star Destroyers aside from the Republic and the loyalist Empire were several independent warlords and the criminal [[Booster Terrik]]—who was Corran Horn's [[Parent/Legends|father]]-in-law—and none of the warlords had claimed to have destroyed Rogue Squadron, the clone correctly realized that the Star Destroyer was the property of Terrik. She believed that Terrik had attempted to find any survivors and that anything he had seen, he must have reported to the Republic. Since Rogue Squadron was facing a foe that outnumbered them six to one, the survival of any of the Rogues would have been broadcast immediately. As rumors spread about Rogue Squadron's destruction, however, Mon Mothma made a public statement on the HoloNet, saying only that she could not tell anything about the squadron's fate in order to not jeopardize the entire Hegemony campaign. Upon hearing the statement, the clone assumed that her plan had succeeded. ===Downfall=== {{Quote|I know you won't reply since you're busy playing dead. Just to let you know, there's one more way I know you're a clone. Isard tried the same trick to escape us on Thyferra. Won't work this time. It's over. Oh, one more thing. Tell Colonel Lorrir he sideslips too much. That's why I got him. And you.|Wedge Antilles, before killing the clone|}} After the Republic conquered Liinade III, it began to send [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] with supplies to the [[Troop/Legends|troops]] [[Garrison/Legends|garrisoned]] there, and Krennel started attacking those convoys. In turn, the clone was analyzing the economic situation on the planets from which the supplies were transferred. When word reached her of another convoy, she did her research and realized that the convoy was only a ruse, created to lure Krennel's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] into a trap. Her conclusion was backed by the fact that a number of New Republic starships were being reported to be "on maneuvers." She relayed the news to Krennel, and the latter planned to use the absence of the Republic ships to make a bold raid on Coruscant itself. During this time, [[Requiem Squadron]], a squadron of TIE Defenders led by a man who introduced himself as Colonel [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Antar Roat]], appeared at Ciutric, expressing their desire to join Krennel's forces. The squadron—which was, in reality, composed by disguised members of Rogue Squadron—was accepted into Krennel's military and placed under the command of Colonel [[Lorrir/Legends|Lorrir]]. The Rogues had allied themselves with the real Isard and infiltrated the planet in order to later bring down Ciutric's [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] and neutralize ground defenses around the [[Ciutric IV prison|prison building]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Daplona]], thus opening the way for Isard's forces to rescue the prisoners. Following the Rogues' instructions, the New Republic force was to engage Krennel's fleet at the same time. When Krennel was ready to move on Coruscant, he sounded a battle alarm. Requiem Squadron was to be transferred aboard one of Krennel's ships in order to be delivered to Coruscant, but Colonel Lorrir suddenly went missing, thus delaying the loading process, as the rest of the squadron was supposed to wait until he headed up first. When Roat had been told not to wait for Lorrir and to get his people airborne, Krennel contacted the clone, asking her to dispatch someone to search for the missing Colonel Lorrir. The clone, however, decided to find Lorrir herself and located him in the operations room inside the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. "Roat," who was in fact the [[Rogue Leader/Legends|leader]] of Rogue Squadron, Wedge Antilles, had [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] Lorrir with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and locked him there. She also found Antilles's fake two-fingered [[Limb/Legends|arm]] and realized his true identity. As the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|Republic fleet]] appeared and [[Battle of Ciutric|engaged]] Krennel's staging ships, Antilles and his men began an attack on the prison; Horn and another Rogue, [[Ooryl Qrygg]], soon liberated the prisoners and began escorting them out of the prison building. [[File:WedgesEntireHead-ROTJ.png|thumb|left|250px|Wedge Antilles, the clone's killer]] Meanwhile, quickly setting her plans for escape in motion, the clone brought Lorrir to his senses and forced him to pilot an [[Imperial Assault Shuttle]] toward the prison, while she took position inside the [[Daplona base training center]]. Patching her [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] through the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle's]] [[Comm unit/Legends|comm unit]], she began talking to Antilles—who was flying cover for Horn and Qrygg in his TIE Defender—in order to appear as though she was aboard the shuttle. As a last resort to deny Rogue Squadron their victory, she ordered Lorrir to destroy the prison facility, and the Colonel fired two [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] at it. One missile was destroyed by Antilles in mid-air, but the other reached its target; however, Antilles had warned his men inside, and they helped the prisoners to brace for the explosion, resulting in no casualties. After the failed attack, Antilles revealed to the clone who she in fact was. She did not believe that she was a mere clone at first, but Antilles ultimately told her of facts that proved it to her. Struck by Antilles's discovery, the clone cried in anger over the comlink. Meanwhile, Antilles, who had participated in training simulations against Lorrir, recognized the Colonel's tendency to sideslip in order to avoid attacks. The leader of Rogue Squadron saw through the clone's ruse, since the real Isard had previously attempted a similar trick to escape from Thyferra. Thus, he signaled two of his Rogues to attack the shuttle from different directions. After [[Myn Donos]] hit the shuttle in the aft port, Lorrir moved to starboard, where [[Gavin Darklighter]] finished him. The damaged shuttle crashed into the [[Daplona River]] and exploded. Playing dead, the clone put static through the comm system. Antilles, however, traced the comm signature to the training center and, after telling the clone that he had deciphered her plan, shot a pair of missiles at the center, killing her. Krennel had been killed in the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] battle, and without a leader, the surviving Hegemony forces surrendered to the Republic. It was soon discovered that the real Isard had planned the entire scheme to take down the clone as a distraction for the Republic, while she—on the orders of the [[Transfer essence|reborn]] Emperor Palpatine—attempted to recover the ''Lusankya'', which had been taken to the [[Bilbringi Shipyards|shipyards]] in the [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]] to undergo repairs after the Battle of Thyferra. With some Imperial troops, Isard [[Mission to Bilbringi|infiltrated]] the ''Lusankya''; Isard went to her quarters, while the Imperials tried to secure the ship. The Imperial's plan, however, had been in turn foreseen by Terrik, and he, along with his daughter [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax]], Sei'lar, and Republic agent [[Iella Wessiri Antilles|Iella Wessiri]], was already waiting for Isard aboard the ''Lusankya''. Terrik lured Isard's men into the auxiliary [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] and captured them, and Wessiri shot Isard in her quarters, killing her. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You are very good at the political manipulation of people—almost as good as I am at killing them. If you confine yourself to what you are good at, I will as well, and our partnership will have a long future.|Delak Krennel|}} As a clone of the real Isard, the clone stood 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and inherited her unique features, such as light skin, black-and-white [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and differently [[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. The left eye was red and the right one was blue. The clone Isard was cunning, manipulative, and ruthless, having inherited these qualities from the real Isard along with her memories and her hatred toward Rogue Squadron. The clone was determined to destroy the squadron at all costs, no matter how many lives it would have cost. She was willing to let the prisoners on Commenor starve to death, and she did not care about Krennel's people when they had not reported after the ambush. In order to lure her enemies into a trap, she weaved a long web of deception, spending vast amounts of time and resources on its creation. Thinking one step ahead of the New Republic, she also provided Krennel with political advice which helped him to rise in popularity and gain support in his fight against the Republic. She often employed a deductive approach, figuring out that the ship that had visited the site of the ambush was Terrik's and connecting the economical situation in the Republic to the upcoming attack on Krennel. The clone preferred to contemplate her next moves sitting in a dark room surrounded by [[Computer/Legends|computer]] monitors. When something did not go according to her plan, she reacted quickly and adjusted to the situation, as was the case when she included the Pulsar Station in her plot to lure Rogue Squadron into a trap. Although scarred as a result of her trauma and the subsequent surgery, the clone was still considered fairly attractive by Krennel. She was also able to sustain her own [[Spy/Legends|spy]] network, which extended as far as Coruscant. She was also self-confident, constantly assuring Krennel that her plans would work, dismissing the New Republic's statement about the Liinade laboratory's connection to Krennel as a hoax, and believing that Rogue Squadron had been destroyed after hearing Mon Mothma's message. Despite all of the clone's planning, however, the real Isard was able to easily see through her schemes simply because the clone had the same mentality as her, and Isard considered her clone's plan to be too complicated. The clone was initially reluctant to believe that she was a mere duplicate, but when Antilles told her facts that proved it, she cried in anger. The fact that she was a clone ultimately resulted in her death as well, as she attempted the shuttle trick that Isard had already used. ==Behind the scenes== The clone of Ysanne Isard was first mentioned in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]], a [[1998]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and illustrated by [[Steve Crespo]] and [[John Nadeau]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/97-771/Star-Wars-X-Wing-Rogue-Squadron-35Mandatory-Retirement-Part-4-of-4|text=STAR WARS: X-WING ROGUE SQUADRON #35}} The clone first appeared in the [[1999]] novel ''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'', also by Stackpole. ==Appearances== *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] {{1stm}} *''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' {{1st}} *[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge (abridged audiobook)|''X-Wing: Isard's Revenge'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Ysanne Isard (clone)}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{FactFile|90|HOR 3-4|Corran Horn}} *{{FactFile|110|ISA 1-4|Ysanne Isard}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{DB|character|ysanneisard|Isard, Ysanne}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |da=Ysanne Isard (klon) |de=Ysanne Isard (Klon) |es=Ysanne Isard (clon) |fi=Ysanne Isard (klooni) |deGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Isard, Ysanne}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Human clones]] [[Category:Imperial warlords]] [[Category:Personnel of Delak Krennel's regime]] [[Category:Personnel of Ysanne Isard's Thyferran regime]]
